title: Broken Stack Overflow Handling: Misbalanced Elements

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers representing the heights of a stack of blocks, where elements with the same height are stacked on top of each other. The task is to find the maximum number of blocks that can fit in the stack without overflowing, assuming that each block can only be placed on top of blocks that are less than or equal to 1/3 of its height. Write a function to determine the maximum number of blocks that can fit in the stack without overflowing.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers representing the heights of a stack of blocks.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum number of blocks that can fit in the stack without overflowing.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n) where n is the number of blocks in the stack.

examples.explanation: The maximum number of blocks that can fit is 3 because we can stack 1, 2, and 3 without overflowing.

hints.text: Use a stack to keep track of the blocks that can fit in the stack.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& blocks); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] blocks); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, blocks: list[int]) -> int: ...

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& blocks) { int count = 0; for (int i = 0; i < blocks.size(); i++) { count = max(count, blocks[i] / 3); } return count; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int solve(int[] blocks) { int count = 0; for (int i = 0; i < blocks.length; i++) { count = Math.max(count, blocks[i] / 3); } return count; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, blocks: list[int]) -> int: count = 0; for i in range(len(blocks)): count = max(count, blocks[i] // 3); return count;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(blocks) { let count = 0; for (let i = 0; i < blocks.length; i++) { count = Math.max(count, blocks[i] / 3); } return count; }

solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(vector<int>& blocks) { stack<int> s; for (int block : blocks) { if (s.empty() || block <= s.top() * 3) s.push(block); else { s.pop(); s.push(block); } } return s.size() + 1; } };
